Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-13-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for Me Faga (administrator).
Paul McGuire appeared by zoom for Kevin Cast (potential heir).

Mrs. McFadden stated that Amilia Iosua is not proceeding with a claim to be an heir of the decedent, that Amilia was not deemed to be a child of the decedent in two child support actions in Sacramento and in San Mateo, and that she received an e-mail from Amilia regarding the above issue. Mr. McGuire stated that he supports the latest petition. Mrs. McFadden also stated that Rosela is an heir, that Mikaio is deemed to be a child of the decedent and that all three would receive an equal one third share. Mrs. McFadden is to file a copy of the e-mail. The Petition for Determination of Entitlement to Estate Distribution is under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition Of Administrator Me Faga To Approve Creditors Claim Of Administrator For Reimbursement Of Funeral Expenses
Off Calendar May-02-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for Me Faga (administrator).
Lydia Smith appeared by zoom for and with Rasela Maugata by zoom.
Paul McGuire appeared by zoom for Devin Cast (potential heir).

Mrs. Smith stated that she filed a proof of service of the order granting the motion to be relieved as counsel, but she is still appearing on the court's record as the attorney. Mrs. McFadden confirmed that there are no written objections to the creditor's claim, and stated that mediation did not go forward, and that she believes there are two valid additional heirs. The Petition to Approve Creditor's Claim of Administrator for Reimbursement Funeral Expenses is under submission. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution is continued to July 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 after the hearing on the petition to determine heirs.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Apr-09-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lydia Smith appeared by zoom for Rasela Maugata (beneficiary).
Paul McGuire appeared by zoom for Devin Cast (potential heir).
Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for Me Faga (administrator).

No one objects to the court relieving the Alvarez Firm. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is under submission with the court's intent to grant the petition upon receipt of a proposed order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Nguyen. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To May-01-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Maureen McFadden, Esq., for Me Faga, Petitioner.

Lydia Smith, Esq., for Rasela Maugata, Beneficiary Objector.

Paul McGuire, Esq., for Devin Cast, Beneficiary Claimant.

At the outset, the court advises that it is issuing an amended minute order referring this matter to mediation with new deadlines as follows: Mediation must be completed no later than Apr-24-2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Apr-29-2024. The Parties are to return on May-01-2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Counsel is instructed to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the order. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the matters on calendar today. Ms. McFadden reports that her client is not bondable at this time and asks fi the court is willing to waive bond. Ms. Smith does not agree with Ms. McFadden's request and offers advocacy for her position. After additional discussion, the court advises that it is not comfortable waiving bond and instead will consider ordering a blocked account. There is discussion re the possible filing of a petition to determine heirship. Mr. McGuire reports that he intends to wait as that matter may be resolved via mediation.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to May-01-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition Of Administrator Me Faga To Approve Creditors Claim Of Administrator For Reimbursement Of Funeral Expenses
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To May-01-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Maureen McFadden, Esq., for Me Faga, Petitioner.

Lydia Smith, Esq., for Rasela Maugata, Beneficiary Objector.

Paul McGuire, Esq., for Devin Cast, Beneficiary Claimant.

At the outset, the court advises that it is issuing an amended minute order referring this matter to mediation with new deadlines as follows: Mediation must be completed no later than Apr-24-2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Apr-29-2024. The Parties are to return on May-01-2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Counsel is instructed to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the order. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the matters on calendar today. Ms. McFadden reports that her client is not bondable at this time and asks fi the court is willing to waive bond. Ms. Smith does not agree with Ms. McFadden's request and offers advocacy for her position. After additional discussion, the court advises that it is not comfortable waiving bond and instead will consider ordering a blocked account. There is discussion re the possible filing of a petition to determine heirship. Mr. McGuire reports that he intends to wait as that matter may be resolved via mediation.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to May-01-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition Of Administrator Me Faga To Approve Creditors Claim Of Administrator For Reimbursement Of Funeral Expenses
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Continued To Feb-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Maureen McFadden, Esq., for and with Me Faga, Administrator.

Lydia Smith, Esq., for Rasela Maugata, Beneficiary.

Paul McGuire, Esq., for Devin Cast, Heir.

Mikaio Maugata, in propria persona, Heir.

Amilia Losua, in propria persona, Heir.

At the outset, the court confirms with Ms. McFadden that there appear to be additional Heirs to this estate, but the legality of the preceding has yet to be determined. Ms. McFadden reports that she has filed an amended petition. Ms. Losua, at the court's query, confirms that she, her brother Mikaio, and Devin Cast are siblings. The Court and Counsel discuss the status of the subject matters. At the court's query, Ms. Smith reports that she intends to file a written objection to the Accounting. Mr. McGuire reports his intention to file a Petition to Determine Heirship. After additional discussion regarding the status of the estate, the court queries counsel regarding taking advantage of the court's pro bono mediation program. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Jan-24-2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Jan-31-2024. The Parties are to return on Feb-07-2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The court recommends that Mikaio and Amilia participate in the mediation with counsel or in propria persona. The court instructs the parties to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the mediation order.

The subject matters are continued to Feb-07-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cabrera. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden, via Zoom, for Me Faga, Petitioner.

The court observes that no one is present to represent the San Francisco Public Guardian's (SFPG) Office and queries counsel if she has heard from the SFPG. Counsel advises that she has not been contacted by the SFPG. The court advises that it is waiving the minor defect in the published notice referring to the instant petition as amended, as an amended has never been filed. The court advises counsel that it has determined that the SFPA has priority for appointment via probate code but since there are no appearances to object, the court announces that it intends to take the matter under submission with the intent to grant the following:

1) Appoint Me Faga as administrator with limited IAEA authority.

2) Bond will not be required at this time but will be required as soon as assets of the estate are determined.

3) Michael Kasolas will be appointed probate referee.

4) A hearing on the status of the filing of a final inventory and appraisal and fixing a bond will be set for December 6, 2022.

5) A hearing on the status of closing the estate will be set for August 8, 2023.

Ms. McFadden is advised a Notice of Improved Financial Situation (Form FW-010) is required from Ms. Faga to determine her continued eligibility for the fee waiver and to determine whether fees previously waived should be reimbursed to the Court under CRC 7.5(d) & (g).

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Ms. VanZandt reports that she plans on filing a competing petition in this matter and that she had attempted to discuss the preceding with the petitioner in this matter and was told not to call him again. Ms. VanZandt believes that a life insurance policy may be the only asset of this estate.

As the petitioner in this matter failed to appear, the petition is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
